title: Business Associate Contracts — BAA Coverage for Connected Systems

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(B) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(B) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.10: Business Associate Contracts — BAA Coverage for Connected Systems.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.10' running on sourcetype epic:interface and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:interface" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen values(interface_name) as ifaces by remote_host
| lookup baa_register.csv hostname as remote_host OUTPUT vendor, baa_status, baa_expiry
| where isnull(baa_status) OR baa_status!="Active"
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_seen)/86400,1)
| sort - last_seen
description: Highlights new interfaces or SaaS tenants talking to ePHI systems without a registered BAA so legal can close contract gaps before production traffic carries PHI.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic and sourcetypes epic:interface, epic:interface`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate `baa_register.csv` from contract repository; (2) integrate ServiceNow vendor risk tasks; (3) block firewall exceptions until BAA recorded (process outside Splunk). Indexes required: `index=epic`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:interface`, `sourcetype=epic:interface``.
visualization: Table (host, vendor, BAA gap), Bar chart (new hosts per week without BAA). Key fields: `days_since`, `ifaces`, `last_seen`, `remote_host`.
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:interface` (remote host, interface name), `lookup baa_register.csv` (`vendor`, `hostname`, `baa_status`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate `baa_register.csv` from contract repository; (2) integrate ServiceNow vendor risk tasks; (3) block firewall exceptions until BAA recorded (process outside Splunk).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:interface" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen values(interface_name) as ifaces by remote_host
| lookup baa_register.csv hostname as remote_host OUTPUT vendor, baa_status, baa_expiry
| where isnull(baa_status) OR baa_status!="Active"
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_seen)/86400,1)
| sort - last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business Associate Contracts — BAA Coverage for Connected Systems (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(B))** — Highlights new interfaces or SaaS tenants talking to ePHI systems without a registered BAA so legal can close contract gaps before production traffic carries PHI.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:interface` (remote host, interface name), `lookup baa_register.csv` (`vendor`, `hostname`, `baa_status`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic; **sourcetype**: epic:interface. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=epic, sourcetype="epic:interface", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by remote_host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(baa_status) OR baa_status!="Active"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (host, vendor, BAA gap), Bar chart (new hosts per week without BAA).
grandmaExplanation: We highlights new interfaces or SaaS tenants talking to ePHI systems without a registered BAA so legal can close contract gaps before production traffic carries PHI. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
